description:
Artocarpus altilis, commonly known as breadfruit, is a tropical to sub-tropical evergreen tree of the mulberry family that typically grows with a spreading crown to 30-50’ (less frequently to 80’) tall. Trunk diameter ranges from 2-6’. All parts of the tree contain a latex milky sap. This tree grows in both seeded and seedless forms, with the commonly marketed cultivars being seedless. Breadfruit is an important food source in many tropical countries.

description: Artocarpus heterophyllus, commonly called jackfruit, is a tropical to subtropical evergreen tree that is native from India to Malaya Peninsula. It is a large, straight-trunked, somewhat stately tree that typically grows 30-70' (sometimes to 90') tall. It is best noted for bearing the largest fruits of any tree in the world. It is considered to be califlorous (flowers and fruits grow on short stems from the trunks and large branches). Fruits are generally melon-shaped, growing from 1-3' long and

description: Ficus elastica, the rubber fig, rubber bush, rubber tree, rubber plant, or Indian rubber bush, Indian rubber tree, or rambung is a species of flowering plant in the family Moraceae, native to eastern parts of South and Southeast Asia. It has become naturalized in Sri Lanka, the West Indies, and the US state of Florida. Its common names reflect its historical use as a source of rubber within its native range, but it is not used in the modern commercial-scale production of natural rubber.

care.water_frequency: Every 7-10 days when top 2 inches are dry
care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, tolerates some direct
care.humidity_action: Wipe leaves with damp cloth regularly to prevent dust buildup.

care.fertilizer_type: Monthly with balanced liquid fertilizer spring-fall

care.toxicity_note: Sap causes skin irritation in pets

Yellowing (overwatering)

description: Ficus lyrata, commonly known as the fiddle-leaf fig, banjo fig, fiddle-leaved fig tree, lyre leaf fig tree, or lyre-leaved fig tree, is a species of plant in the mulberry and fig family Moraceae. It is native to western Africa, but is cultivated around the world as an ornamental plant. It has received the Royal Horticultural Society's Award of Garden Merit.
